combat_history.event: First operational deployment of J-15s during Liaoning's transit through Taiwan Strait and operations near Taiwan. Multiple J-15s conducted flight operations demonstrating China's carrier aviation capability.
combat_history.significance: Marked the operational debut of Chinese carrier aviation, signaling China's growing naval power projection capabilities in the first island chain

combat_history.event: J-15s conducted sustained flight operations from Liaoning during major naval exercises in South China Sea, including simulated strike missions and air defense patrols.
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated China's ability to project air power from carriers in contested waters, directly challenging US naval dominance in the region

combat_history.event: Regular J-15 operations from both Liaoning and Shandong during increased tensions with Taiwan, including flights that crossed the median line of Taiwan Strait.
combat_history.significance: First use of carrier-based aircraft in direct pressure campaigns against Taiwan, representing escalation in China's military coercion tactics

modernization.description: Development of catapult-capable variant for Type 003 Fujian carrier with electromagnetic catapult system. Includes reinforced airframe, modified landing gear, and enhanced payload capacity.
modernization.impact: Will significantly increase operational flexibility and payload capacity, bringing J-15 closer to Western carrier fighter capabilities

modernization.description: Upgrading existing J-15A fleet with improved radar processing, enhanced electronic warfare capabilities, and better datalink integration with PLAN combat systems.
modernization.impact: Improves situational awareness and integration with carrier battle group operations

modernization.description: Potential retrofit of improved WS-10 variants or new engine types to increase thrust and reliability, addressing current power limitations.
modernization.impact: Could partially address ski-jump takeoff weight limitations and improve overall performance

vulnerabilities.description: Current J-15s operating from Liaoning and Shandong are severely weight-limited due to ski-jump takeoffs, typically carrying only 60% of maximum payload with full fuel load.
vulnerabilities.context: Fundamental limitation that prevents full utilization of aircraft capability, particularly problematic for long-range strike missions or heavy air-to-air loadouts.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: J-15T variant being developed for catapult operations on Type 003 carrier, but existing fleet remains constrained

vulnerabilities.description: WS-10H engines have historically shown lower reliability than Russian AL-31 equivalents, with power output insufficient for optimal carrier operations at maximum weight.
vulnerabilities.context: Engine issues limit operational availability and contribute to takeoff weight restrictions, reducing overall combat effectiveness.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Ongoing engine development programs and potential future engine upgrades planned

vulnerabilities.description: Large radar cross-section and lack of stealth features make J-15 highly vulnerable to modern surface-to-air missiles and advanced fighter aircraft.
vulnerabilities.context: Against peer adversaries with advanced air defenses and stealth fighters, J-15 survivability is questionable in contested airspace.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Electronic warfare variants and standoff weapons being developed, but fundamental design limitations remain

vulnerabilities.description: PLAN carrier aviation is still developing operational expertise, with limited pilot experience in complex carrier operations and combat scenarios.
vulnerabilities.context: Carrier aviation requires extensive training and experience that takes decades to develop fully, giving established carrier operators significant advantages.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Intensive training programs underway, but experience gap will persist for years

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Integrated air wing component for PLAN carrier battle groups, primarily air superiority and fleet defense with secondary strike capability
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates as part of carrier air wing with 24-36 J-15s alongside helicopters and support aircraft within carrier strike group
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Aircraft availability rates reportedly lower than Western equivalents due to maintenance challenges and engine reliability issues

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Super Hornet has superior payload flexibility due to catapult launches, more advanced avionics and sensors, but J-15 has larger airframe with potentially longer range. Super Hornet has extensive combat experience advantage.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: How China's first carrier fighter stacks up against America's proven naval workhorse

peer_comparisons.key_differences: F-35C has overwhelming advantages in stealth, sensor fusion, and situational awareness, but J-15 carries heavier payloads and has longer range. Represents different design philosophies - stealth vs payload.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Stealth vs Payload: Can China's Flying Shark compete with America's invisible carrier fighter?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: J-15 based heavily on Su-33 design but incorporates Chinese avionics and weapons systems. Both share ski-jump limitations, but J-15 has more modern electronics and indigenous weapon integration.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Copy or Evolution: How China transformed Russia's carrier fighter into the Flying Shark

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Rafale M is smaller but more agile with superior multirole capability and advanced sensors. J-15 has longer range and larger payload capacity. Both operate from non-US carriers but with different launch systems.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East meets West: Comparing China's Flying Shark to France's naval Rafale

peer_comparisons.key_differences: MiG-29K is smaller and more maneuverable but with less range and payload than J-15. Both designed for ski-jump operations, but different size categories and mission emphasis.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Russian Rivals: How China's J-15 compares to the MiG-29K carrier fighter
